# tape-six-fast-check

> Property-based testing plugin for tape-six powered by fast-check. Registers two tester methods ‚Äî `t.prop()` (properties) and `t.scheduler()` (race conditions) ‚Äî each reporting one aggregate counted assertion with structured counterexample/seed/path data read from `fc.check()` run details (never parsed from text). Peer dependencies: tape-six ^1.16.0, fast-check ^4.

## Install

npm install --save-dev tape-six-fast-check tape-six fast-check

## Quick start

```js
import test from 'tape-six';
import fc from 'fast-check';
import 'tape-six-fast-check'; // side-effect import registers t.prop / t.scheduler

test('addition is commutative', async t => {
  await t.prop([fc.integer(), fc.integer()], (a, b) => a + b === b + a, 'a + b === b + a');
});
```

## API

### await t.prop(arbitraries, predicate, options?, msg?)

Runs `fc.check()` over the property and reports one aggregate assertion on the current test. Returns the fast-check `RunDetails`.

- `arbitraries` ‚Äî non-empty array of fast-check arbitraries, one per predicate argument.
- `predicate` ‚Äî `(...values) => boolean | void | Promise<boolean | void>`; **throw or return `false` to fail**, anything else passes; sync or async. Do not call `t.*` assertions inside ‚Äî the predicate runs up to `numRuns` times.
- `options` ‚Äî `fc.check()` parameters (`numRuns`, `seed`, `path`, `endOnFailure`, ‚Ä¶). The message may take this slot: `t.prop(arbs, predicate, 'name')`.
- `msg` ‚Äî assertion name; defaults to `'property holds'`.

On failure the assertion's `actual` carries `{counterexample, seed, path, numRuns, numShrinks, error}`. Replay: pass `{seed, path, endOnFailure: true}` in `options`.

Invalid arguments (empty/non-array arbitraries, non-function predicate) reject with `TypeError`.

### await t.scheduler(body, options?, msg?)

Race-condition sugar over `fc.asyncProperty(fc.scheduler(), body)`; same reporting and replay mechanics.

- `body` ‚Äî `(s: Scheduler) => boolean | void | Promise<boolean | void>`; wrap promises with `s.schedule(...)` / `s.scheduleFunction(...)`, drain with `await s.waitAll()`; throw or return `false` to fail.
- `options` ‚Äî `fc.check()` parameters plus optional `act`, forwarded to `fc.scheduler({act})`.
- `msg` ‚Äî assertion name; defaults to `'scheduled property holds'`.

## TypeScript

`index.d.ts` augments tape-six's `Tester` interface (`declare module 'tape-six'`), so both methods are typed after the side-effect import; predicate argument types are inferred from the arbitraries tuple. Exported helper types: `ArbitraryTuple<Ts>`, `PropPredicate<Ts>`, `SchedulerBody`, `SchedulerOptions`.

## Notes

- ES modules only; no build step; no runtime dependencies (both integrations are peers).
- Always `await` the calls ‚Äî the assertion reports when the run completes.
- Browser: use tape-six's import-map setup (tape-six wiki: "Set-up tests", "Environment ‚Äê Browsers") and add entries for `tape-six-fast-check` (`/node_modules/tape-six-fast-check/index.js`), `fast-check` (`/node_modules/fast-check/lib/fast-check.js`), and the seven `pure-rand` subpaths fast-check imports (import maps don't apply `exports` tables) ‚Äî or replace the fast-check + pure-rand entries with one CDN entry: `"fast-check": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/fast-check@4/+esm"`. Full map: README ¬ß In the browser.
- Docs: https://github.com/uhop/tape-six-fast-check#readme
